Canadian sailor Sarah Douglas has gone viral after providing a sneak peek into the welcome packs gifted to athletes in the ParisOlympicvillage.

Sharing a video toTikTok, the 30-year-old offered a mini-tour of her room — featuring the infamous cardboard single beds thought to deter morale-boosting under the sheets.

The two-time Olympian began the clip with the caption, “What every Olympic athlete receives in their room”, and continued to provide a close-up of each item.

If you’d like to view this content, please adjust yourCookie Settings.

To find out more about how we use cookies, please see ourCookie Guide.

Among the list of inclusions were water bottles, a toiletry bag, and a bespoke golden Samsung flip phone with an Olympic message engraved which Douglas unpacked in a later video.

And of course, a supply of condoms were provided.

Displaying one blue and pink wrapper, cute messages emblazoned across the packaging caught the athlete’s eye.

“On the field of love, play fair (ask for consent)“, one read.

The other insisted, “No need to be a gold medallist to wear it!”

Garnering more than four million views, followers of the sailor had some questions.

“They have antisex beds but give condoms?😭”, one person asked.

“I love how they made anti-sex beds but still give u condoms bc they know it’ll still happen,” another joked.

Games chiefs toldSky Newsin March that they planned to reunite competitors following the COVID-induced intimacy ban that plagued the Tokyo Games, providing enough prophylactics for each athlete to get back on the horse twice a day.

“Working with the athletes commission, we wanted to create some places where the athletes would feel very enthusiastic and comfortable,” Laurent Michaud, director of the village told the publication.

The Olympic Village is said to be the single most expensive project at the Paris Games at $3.3 billion — largely funded by property investors.

title: Athlete reveals contents of Paris Olympic Village welcome back, featuring condoms and golden smartphone | PerthNow
description: Canadian sailor Sarah Douglas has gone viral after providing a sneak peek into the welcome packs gifted to athletes in the Paris Olympic village.
